programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Four proud BandB owners do battle to be named the best value for money. The competition kicks off at Greenways of Malvern in Worcestershire, where Geri Atterbury likes to provide a home from home.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to The Royal Forester at Bewdley, Worcestershire, where couple Sean McGahern and Maxine Parker want to wow their guests.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to Ferry Lodge in Holyhead, North Wales, where mother and son duo Sarah and Luke Allaway hope to give their guests their very best.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The last visit of the week is to Court Colman Manor, Bridgend, where partners Sanjeev Bhagotra and Elinor Roberts have been running the family business for four years.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Its the final day and the BandBers meet one last time to settle some scores and find out how much theyve been paid. After some hosting roasting and dust disputes, the winner is announced.

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The weeks last visit is to Valley View in Pendle, Lancashire where sisters Sharon and Deby hope the BandBers get onboard with glamping. But one guest thinks his pod is like a student flat...

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Its payment day, and after a competition full of controversial comments and divisive opinions, the BandBers have plenty of scores to settle. But whos won the week?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: In Hastings, partners James and Elaine scour 15 charity shops, hunting for vintage gems and a hidden star buy before facing a tense, live online auction for charity.

programme.title: Designing the Hebrides
programme.desc: Banjo has been commissioned by fish shop manager and friend Sally, who wants to modernise her familys traditional business in Tobermory. Banjo has bold plans to utterly transform both the inside and outside of the shop, but Sally is worried that her family will disapprove of the radical makeover. With his band of trusty locals, including Eoghann the carpenter and handyman Tom, Banjo takes huge risks in realising his design ambitions.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Its all go in rural Oxfordshire as Richie Anderson helps a couple branch out and leave the suburbs to fulfil their dreams of creating a forest school. With an impressive budget, the stunning property line-up is full of leafy promise, including a beautiful, converted mill house thats anything but run-of-the-mill. Richie also meets an innovative young artist who creates her masterpieces on a bicycle-powered sewing machine... artistry on a wheelie different level!

programme.title: I Made It at Market
programme.desc: Dom revisits upholsterer Hayley and stone carver Richard to find out how they have built money-making businesses after leaving his business bootcamp and what they are earning now.

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp, with sidekick Sarah Greene, hosts the quiz show where knowledge equals speed. Lee, Nadine, Tom, Carol and Albert race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try to win £5,000.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Tom Allen for the brainy quiz where the contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Tir goes even further into the underbelly of Las Vegas to investigate how the reinvention of the city to pull in a new generation of visitors is affecting those who live and work there.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Sex, Bribes and Murder
programme.desc: After a three-minute video featuring a female prison officer and an inmate having sex inside HMP Wandsworth goes viral, award-winning journalist Mobeen Azhar begins an investigation into what happened and why. A year of twists, turns and damning revelations pulls back the curtain on a dangerous wider world of coercion, corruption and contraband thriving behind bars.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Sex, Bribes and Murder
programme.desc: As Mobeens investigation deepens, it becomes clearer that illicit relationships between prisoners and jail staff are just the tip of the iceberg. The bigger story involves organised criminal gangs determined to cash in on a lucrative prison drug economy. When news breaks of a prison governor being jailed for a relationship with a drug-dealing gang boss, Mobeen becomes determined to uncover the true extent of criminal influence inside our prisons.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve combs the Venezuelan wetlands looking for the biggest snake in the world, the mighty Green Anaconda, capable of growing to over six metres in length. He searches within for a true toxic terror, the Scolopendra, a giant centipede armed with a powerful venomous bite.

programme.desc: Coco is showing Bing how magnets work. While Coco builds a tower, Bing wants to find more magnetic things. programme.title: Go Jetters

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica is broke and decides to gamble on the stock market, and Chandler and Ross run into a couple of bullies in Central Park. Meanwhile, Rachel and Joey help Phoebe locate her father, but a vicious dog prevents Phoebe from finally meeting him. After accidentally injuring the dog with her car, Phoebe gets to know her fathers second wife whom she never knew about, and another long-lost family member.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica decides to hold a surprise birthday for Rachel, but everything goes wrong when Rachels estranged parents turn up unexpectedly. The party then splits into two factions: a dreary gathering in Monicas apartment, and a college-style drunken party at Chandler and Joeys flat.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where No Ones Ready
programme.desc: In a race against the clock, Ross tries to get the gang dressed and ready for a black-tie benefit at the museum.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica decides the best way to get over Richard is to keep herself busy. She develops a `jam plan, hoping the distraction of cooking will divert her attention. After making jars and jars of jam Monica decides she still wants to have a baby, so she begins searching for a sperm donor, and comes across Joeys file. Elsewhere, Phoebe is mistakenly stalked by a man who thinks shes Ursula, and Chandler receives dating advice.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: When Janice asks whether any of the friends have ever dated one another the gang has a flashback to three years ago (one year before the pilot was supposed to have happened) when everyones lives were beginning to come together. Phoebe moves out of Monicas apartment, Chandler searches for a new roommate - ending up with Joey, Monica bumps into Rachel, and Ross learns that Carol is a lesbian.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: On Thanksgiving, the friends form teams and play American football. Ross and Monica relive their sibling rivalry by going head-to-head as opposing team captains. Rachel is upset that Ross didnt choose her for his team, and Joey tries to outwit his foe Chandler. Meanwhile, the turkey is left stewing while the losing team calls for a rematch.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Without the Ski Trip
programme.desc: Ross and Rachel have been fighting for the groups attention since the break up. Rachel invites everyone except Ross to her family cabin in the mountains, but on the way to the cabin, they run out of petrol. Everyone wants to call Ross for help, but Rachel wont let them.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Hypnosis Tape
programme.desc: Phoebe is upset when her 18-year-old half-brother, Frank Jr, wants to marry his attractive home economics teacher, who is more than 25 years older than him. Elsewhere, Monica receives a tip from an admirer worth a small fortune, and a hypnosis tape intended to assist Chandler give up smoking has a rather strange effect on him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Tiny T-Shirt
programme.desc: Rachels former colleague Mark asks her out on a date. When Rachel invites Ross to her apartment, he thinks she wants to get back together, but she just wants to return his things. Later, as Rachel and Mark return from their date, Ross spies on them through the peephole of Joey and Chandlers door. Meanwhile, Monica dates a millionaire, and Joey is cast in a play and clashes with the female lead.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With a Chick and a Duck
programme.desc: When Monicas millionaire friend Pete buys a restaurant and asks Monica to be the head chef, she is faced with a difficult decision. Meanwhile, Ross makes a sacrifice for Rachel, and Joey buys Chandler an unusual pet.

programme.title: New Girl
programme.desc: Jess fears someone is trying to sabotage her first middle school dance and she enlists the gang to help her chaperone on the big night.

programme.title: New Girl

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Baby on the Bus
programme.desc: Joey and Chandler babysit Ben, who turns out to be a babe-magnet until they accidentally leave him on a bus. Meanwhile, Phoebe loses her job as the singer at Central Perk to Stephanie.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Finds Out
programme.desc: Ross and Julie announce they are getting a cat together, while Rachel gets drunk on a date and all she talks about is Ross. She decides to get closure by calling him and telling his machine she is over him. Meanwhile, Chandler regrets asking Monica to help him lose weight.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross agonises over whether he should stay with Julie or date Rachel. The gang encourage him to compare the two, so he starts with a list of Rachels negative qualities. Unfortunately, Rachel sees the list.

programme.desc: A gruff cop turned bounty hunter captures a fugitive accountant accused of fraud and sets off from New York for Los Angeles to collect his prize. However, he is unaware that both the Mafia and the FBI are also on the accountants trail and hot on their heels.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Barry and Mindys Wedding
programme.desc: When Rachel serves as a bridesmaid at the wedding of her ex-fiance Barry and her friend Mindy, she relives the nightmare of her own failed wedding day. Meanwhile, Chandler becomes enamoured with a woman he meets on the internet, but is surprised when he finally meets her.

programme.title: Midnight Run
programme.desc: A gruff cop turned bounty hunter captures a fugitive accountant accused of fraud and sets off from New York for Los Angeles to collect his prize. However, he is unaware that both the Mafia and the FBI are also on the accountants trail and hot on their heels.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats

Owner Laura ran an incredibly successful catering business for years before transferring her skills to running a restaurant, but she has failed to grasp that the two things are very different beasts.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travel to a `high end husband and wife run Bistro in Lighthouse Point, Florida. Stubborn Scottish dictatorial chef and owner Andy has cooked for celebrated chefs in the past, but since opening Le Bistro has lost his passion for cooking and doesnt listen to anyone. With customers grading his food 3/10, and Andy not responding well to criticism, can Gordon get him to listen?

programme.desc: Indecisive groom Dele has £12,000 and just 3 weeks to put on a wedding to impress Bride-to-be Tracy, but after a 15-year engagement Tracy wants nothing less than her dream of a romantic wedding in a castle.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Comic book loving groom Anthony has just three weeks and £12,000 to plan a wedding for his bride-to-be Lauren. While Anthony wants to prove he can be a real superhero to Lauren with a comic inspired wedding in a cave, she is dreaming of a more traditional affair.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon is at Sushi Ko in California. Owner Akira was a successful sushi chef at someone elses restaurant so he and his wife Lisa decided to branch out on their own. Luca shares these adventures with his newfound best friend, but all the fun is threatened by a deeply held secret: he is a sea monster from another world just below the waters surface.

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: The Mark of Zorro
programme.desc: Feckless Mexican nobleman Don Diego returns to his family home to discover the locality has been taken over by corrupt officials. Consequently, he adopts the disguise of Zorro, masked master swordsman, who aims to clear the land of corruption.

programme.title: Cheaper by the Dozen 2
programme.desc: Tom Baker and wife Kate, hoping to bring their family together for a memorable summer vacation, take their twelve offspring to the picturesque Lake Winnetka. Their retreat soon becomes a place of cut-throat one-upmanship when they enter into a competition with the over-achieving members of a large family headed by Toms long-time rival, Jimmy Murtaugh.

programme.title: Dantes Peak
programme.desc: Dantes Peak has just been pronounced the second best place in the world to live. Will this title be affected by the danger of the volcano which looms over it? Volcanologist Harry Dalton comes to the town to study the volcano, which he believes to be about to blow. His only ally is the towns mayor, Rachel Wando, whose family is one of those at risk.

programme.title: Gemini Man
programme.desc: Henry Brogan is an elite assassin who becomes the target of a mysterious operative who can seemingly predict his every move. To his horror, he soon learns that the man whos trying to kill him is a younger, faster, cloned version of himself.

programme.title: Tombstone
programme.desc: Gripping account of the battle for law and order in the Old West based on the story of US marshal Wyatt Earp, his friend Doc Holliday and the gunfight at the OK Corral. The Earp brothers ride into the town of Tombstone intent on settling down, but when a murderous gang known as the Cowboys begins terrorising the citizens, a showdown is inevitable.

programme.title: The Predator
programme.desc: From the outer reaches of space to the small-town streets of suburbia, the hunt comes home. The universes most lethal hunters are stronger, smarter and deadlier than ever before, having genetically upgraded themselves with DNA from other species. When a boy accidentally triggers their return to Earth, only a ragtag crew of ex-soldiers and an evolutionary biologist can prevent the end of the human race.

programme.title: The Mustang
programme.desc: A violent convict is given the chance to participate in a rehabilitation therapy program centered around the training of wild mustangs.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guys sampling foods straight off the grill. In Los Angeles, theres a burger joint spicing things up with a hot pork sandwich, and going Mediterranean with a lamb burger.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guys hitting the road with a couple of his A-list friends. In Georgetown, Texas, Guy hooks up with Matthew McConaughey at a massive diner serving up ham and waffles.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Neapolitan pizza, spicy chickpea and oxtail stews, and chicken burgers and quail.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guys got a line on some righteous sandwiches. In Austin, Texas, the gourmet sandwich shop is slicing up duck pastrami and a knuckle sandwich.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey sails to Philadelphia to tackle the epic Classic Whale Challenge, a 6 kg bagel sandwich stuffed with cream cheese, nova salmon, whitefish, tomatoes and onions.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey heads to Los Angeles, where he samples a burger crossed with a pizza. Then, he takes on the Zombie Burrito challenge, during which he tries to demolish a burrito laden with ghost pepper-fuelled death sauce and zombie sauce.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey Webb heads to Pittsburgh for a five-pound sandwich loaded with meat, cheese, fries, and coleslaw.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey Webb ventures to beautiful Boise, Idaho, for a sushi and soup challenge so diabolically spicy that it goes by the name of Demons Delight.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey Webb travels over the Ozarks to tackle the Goliath, a massive cupcake equal to 22 regular-sized cupcakes.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey travels to Seattle where he climbs a Mount Fuji-sized burger and takes on seven terrifyingly spicy Buffalo wings that have defeated over 5,000 challengers.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey Webb travels to San Diego to face off against a stack of pancakes layered with bacon, eggs, sausage, country fried steak, and thick cut ham.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Caseys delicious mission brings him to Daytona Beach, Florida, where he takes on a mammoth three-tier sandwich stacked with two burgers, six pieces of mahi mahi, and two pieces of Texas toast.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey Webb travels to Grand Rapids, Michigan, where he takes on a five-pound tray loaded with chili dogs, French fries, beer-battered onion rings and a 20-ounce soda.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Caseys mouthwatering mission brings him to Tennessee, where he faces his hottest challenge yet, three jumbo chicken tenders smothered in a Carolina Reaper-infused sauce.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James returns home to his native Yorkshire, where he is joined by good friend and multi award-winning chef Brian Turner. James visits top chef Frances Atkins beautiful restaurant the Yorke Arms, where he enjoys her signature dish al fresco, in her unusual dining room the Onion. Brian tastes the delights of some Yorkshire charcuterie, and later they both take time out, where James brushes up his clay pigeon skills and Brian is challenged to some off-road driving.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James visits the stunning Lake District, where he is joined by top Michelin-starred chef Clare Smyth. James visits Clares meat suppliers the Lake District Farmers, while Clare learns the secret of the Lake Districts famous sticky toffee pudding. They both visit the Windermere Jetty Museum, and are treated to a trip out on Lake Windermere on a traditional steam-powered boat.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its the semi-final and the remaining bakers take on more challenges set by judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith in the nations most famous tent, for a place in the final.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The remaining bakers take on the final challenges set by judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith, as they compete to be crowned Bake Off 2021 champion.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Bake Off is back as 12 new bakers enter the iconic white tent. Its cake week, and the bakers tackle a striking sponge and a scaled-down version of a home close to their hearts.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its Biscuit Week, and the bakers kick off with illusion macarons in the Signature, before producing a fruity favourite in the Technical. And in the Showstopper, they put their creativity to the test by making a 3D mask entirely from biscuits.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Britain
programme.desc: Foodie Adam Richman embarks on a unique culinary tour of Britain, visiting places that share their name with a famous food, from Sandwich to Scone.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Britain
programme.desc: Foodie Adam Richman embarks on a unique culinary tour of Britain, visiting places that share their name with a famous food, from Sandwich to Scone.

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: Jamie makes lasagne with roasted duck, the ultimate bacon sarnie and, from rural Mexico, huevos rancheros - beautifully cooked eggs with a spicy tomato salsa, black beans, avocado and a soft tortilla.

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: Jamie makes fish and chips with an Indian pale ale batter. Theres a treat for vegetarians with Jamies version of the Indonesian salad gado gado and lastly theres a speedy crab linguine.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined in Northern Ireland by his good pal and local boy Paul Rankin. They brave the Carrick-a-Rede rope bridge together, then James cooks a tasty fish dish with the dramatic coastline as his backdrop. Theres a fishing trip over to Rathlin Island on the menu too, where James tastes some dishes made from the local kelp. James and Paul then visit the Old Lammas Fair, where they taste some of the delicious seafood.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James returns to Northern Ireland with his old chef buddy Paul Rankin, where theyre exploring the area around Lough Neagh, the biggest freshwater lake in the British Isles. James and Paul discover a wasabi farm, a rarity outside of Japan. Next, Paul visits an old friend, a local honey producer, while James cooks overlooking the dramatic backdrop of Slieve Gullion: the highest point in Armagh.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guys checking out some unique combos. In Korea Town, Los Angeles, Guy visits a craft beer joint putting duck into their fries as well as in the French dip.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guys pulling right up to the window and digging in. In Austin, Texas, theres a tiny trailer serving up home made sandwiches to customers on the street and in the bar next door.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: A pirates barbecue; twist on hot wings; twist on Asian, Mexican and Italian; chicken dumplings in a hot-dog joint.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy is set on food with international influences, including schwarma at a Lebanese joint.

programme.desc: En route to completing his journey at John OGroats, Rick enjoys freshly cooked lobster on Crail harbour front and obtains oatmeal from a watermill in the Tay Valley to make the famous Scottish dessert Cranachan.

programme.desc: Jamie make Sicilian-inspired meatballs and tomato `Norma sauce. Theres also Jamies quick chicken wrap, a silky carbonara of mackerel, and tips on how to get the best out of fresh herbs.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver proves that frozen fish can be just as good as fresh with his stunning fish tikka curry. Hes got two brilliant ideas for making tasty mid-week suppers out of the leftover meat from Sundays roast too.

programme.desc: Ina is cooking up a fantastic meal with her friend Antonia. She makes gazpacho with goats cheese croutons, mustard marinated flank steak and Sicilian grilled swordfish.

programme.title: James Martins Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James culinary adventure hits the South West of England with a visit to the food capital of this region, the vibrant city of Bristol. Here he meets up with local chef Romy Gill who gives James a guided tour of this incredible place. Their first stop is Wapping Wharf, a series of shipping containers housing everything from a butcher to a cheese-monger. After a bit of shopping, James pitches up his mobile kitchen overlooking the iconic Clifton Suspension Bridge to make a tagine of lamb chops.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Jamie make Sicilian-inspired meatballs and tomato `Norma sauce. Theres also Jamies quick chicken wrap, a silky carbonara of mackerel, and tips on how to get the best out of fresh herbs.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver proves that frozen fish can be just as good as fresh with his stunning fish tikka curry. Hes got two brilliant ideas for making tasty mid-week suppers out of the leftover meat from Sundays roast too.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina is cooking up a fantastic meal with her friend Antonia. She makes gazpacho with goats cheese croutons, mustard marinated flank steak and Sicilian grilled swordfish.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina Gartens sharing pot luck party secrets with local farmers Katie and Amanda, who are throwing a start of season bash.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The search is on to crown Britains `Best Amateur Baker.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The search is on to crown Britains `Best Amateur Baker.

programme.title: Marcus in the Med: Mallorca
programme.desc: In search of the ultimate light bite, Marcus visits Port de Soller to try their famous red prawns and gets a masterclass in ham in the market.

programme.title: Marcus in the Med: Mallorca
programme.desc: Marcus Wareing immerses himself in Mallorcan culture and cooking as he discovers delicious new dishes and hidden restaurants on this stunning island.

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Heroes
programme.desc: In Wakefield, Rick meets Janet Oldroyd, whose family have been harvesting rhubarb for the past four generations, and they whip up a tasty rhubarb crumble. Then in Bradford, he discovers the perfect recipe for lamb karahi at a Karachi restaurant.

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Heroes
programme.desc: Rick and his little hairy friend, Chalky, pay a visit to Ricks favourite formal vegetable garden, at Chatsworth House. He crosses the River Severn to sample the delights of Wales, including cawl and Welsh Black cattle.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guys traveling all over the country searching for a taste of the South. In Vancouver, British Columbia, Southern staples are served with a twist, like the pulled pork pancakes with a side of Jack Daniels syrup. In Charleston, South Carolina, the local football legend is cranking out some real deal comfort food with favourites like sweet tea-marinated pork chops and deep-fried hot dogs topped with chili.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, some of Guys favourites are totally homemade from coast to coast. In Manhattan, New York, a real deal chef is cranking out homemade pretzels with a special beer cheese sauce. In Vancouver, British Columbia, the butcher shop turned bakery and diner is serving up standards like burgers and turkey pot pie. And in Santa Barbara, California, the family-run joint scratch making authentic Mexican food.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guy goes all animal. In Charleston, South Carolina, the husband and wife duo flying high for all things bird, like a duck club with homemade garlic mayo. In Queens, New York, a Cuban family cranking out totally traditional steak and chicken dishes, like ropa vieja and arroz con pollo.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guys tasting all kinds of international comfort classics. In Santa Barbara theres an authentic British fish and chips joint known for keeping it real - from the haggis to the toad in the hole.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey Webb journeys across America to tackle daunting food challenges and sample iconic regional dishes. Enormous food options create problems for even the most voracious of eaters.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey Webb journeys across America to tackle daunting food challenges and sample iconic regional dishes. Enormous food options create problems for even the most voracious of eaters.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: Buddy creates a cake for the Tribeca Film Festival with a popcorn-flavoured filling. Plus, a long-time customers return to place a special order.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: Buddy learns of Ralphs secret life when he is asked to make a cake of a mythical griffin. Plus, Buddy creates a worldly cake for foreign exchange students.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two chefs are cooking for judges Matthew Fort, Prue Leith and Oliver Peyton for the very first time. Joining them as guest judge is John Williams MBE, the executive head chef at The Ritz, the only hotel to hold a royal warrant for banqueting.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is Chris McGowans third time however, he is facing tough competition from Mark Abbott, head chef at Daniel Cliffords 2 Michelin star restaurant, Midsummer House, and also from Eddie Attwell who has just completed a year as head chef at Ardtara Country House in Upperlands.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Mark Abbott is pushing himself again plating up a complex fish course of the very finest produce. He is calling it Shellsational but at the last minute he forgets a key element. Returning chef Chris McGowan is refining cod and chips and Eddie Attwell is getting classical with the king of the sea, turbot.The repartee is relentless as the chefs jostle for top place on the leader board. Once again there is plenty of emotion as the veteran chef delivers the scores.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the main course and returning Chef Chris McGowan is feeling confident but part of his dish involves making his own venison sausages to serve with a venison rack, and getting it all done in time is straining his emotions. Eddie Attwell is also serving the meat but with a traditional Northern Irish dish called Dock pudding. Mark Abbott is pushing himself again, he is using wagyu beef and serving it with tongue and corn cooked in a variety of ways.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three chefs from Northern Ireland are cooking their desserts and one chef is on course for a historic score, he could beat his boss - a veteran of GBM - and achieve more marks than anyone has done throughout the history of the competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the head to head. The two highest scoring chefs from Northern Ireland are cooking for judges Oliver Peyton, Prue Leith and Matthew Fort who are joined by guest judge, restaurant critic and Editor-at-Large of the Independent, Amol Rajan.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the national finals of `Great British Menu. Before cooking starts the eight champions are called to Londons Southbank to get a sight of the prize, the chance to cook at a banquet held at the Palace of Westminster, in celebration of everyday Great Britons honoured by the Queen. It has a huge impact on every one of the chefs, each is determined to do all he can to get a dish to the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its day two of the national finals, and the eight champions cook their fish courses. This years challenge has been to produce dishes that are a lasting legacy to the New Elizabethan Age. They must showcase the transformation in British cuisine during the Queens historic 64 year reign.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is main course day, the one course every chef wants to cook at the banquet. Judging the chefs dishes are Matthew Fort, Prue Leith and Oliver Peyton and guest judge John Williams MBE, executive chef of The Ritz.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the final day of the competition, and the last chance for the eight champions to get one of their dishes to the banquet for Queen Elizabeth lls Great Britons. Judges Matthew Fort, Prue Leith and Oliver Peyton will judge the dessert course and then decide on the banquet menu to be served at the Houses of Parliament. Joining them in the judging chamber is Grace Dent, restaurant critic, journalist and author.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The winning chefs arrive at the Palace of Westminster to cook for the Banquet of Great Britons. The chefs have been selected because their dishes showcase contemporary British cuisine and demonstrate the transformation in British food during the Queens historic reign.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Competing for top spot are Danny Parker, head chef at Michelin starred `House of Tides in Newcastle, who is back to try his luck in the competition again after being knocked out in the regional heat last year following a tense tie-break. Hes taking on two newcomers: Dan Fletcher, protege of Phil Howard, and now head chef at `Fenchurch in the City of London, and David Coulson,who runs `Peace and Loaf in Newcastle, which boasts 2 AA rosettes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: After a tense starter course the chefs are all aiming for 10s with their unique fish dishes. David is celebrating a personal connection to the brief with his `My Cod I Bet Me Maam Has Seen Some Trotters, a tribute to his mum who has worked as a chiropodist. Dans `For All Those Sleepless Nights pays tribute to nightshift workers from the NHS with some bold but classic flavours while Dannys `Not All Superheroes Wear Capes aims to give a taste of luxury to the hardworking staff.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dan pays tribute to an icon of modern nursing, Florence Nightingale, while Danny in inspired by the founder of the NHS, Aneurin Bevan. Davids dish is dedicated to the air ambulance staff who saved the life of his fiance, and he even takes a trip to meet one of them.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the chefs last chance to impress and tensions are high. Dan takes a risk with an unusual combination of ewes milk and bergamot while Danny is determined to pull out all the stops with his complex dessert `Aunty Gwens Chocolate. David hopes to transform a childhood comfort classic into a show stopping dessert. Dan also takes a trip to meet Dr Ben Clarke who was working in the Royal London Hospital AandE during the London Bridge terror attack.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North East chefs must cook their four course menus again, but will they take on board their veterans advice as they serve their `Feast To Say Thank You?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Banquet champion Selin Kiazim, a rising star of the London scene renowned for her Turkish Cypriot fusion flavours, is back hoping to join the elite group of chefs who have made it to the banquet twice. She is up against James Cochran whos worked at some of Londons toughest kitchens including 2 Michelin starred `The Ledbury and Scott Goss from `The Twenty Six in Tunbridge Wells, renowned for his use of local Kent ingredients.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs do battle for the fish course and the atmosphere in the kitchen is tense. Lobster is catch of the day for James whose dish is Caribbean-inspired. Scott is attempting to elevate a classic fish pie, dedicated to his mum who worked as a pharmacist in the NHS for 23 years. And Selin is taking a risk with a complex three-part fish dish featuring sardines, scallops and langoustines, as a nod to the purported health benefits of a Mediterranean diet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scott is showcasing rare breed Tamworth pork to say thank you to the rare breed of people who work for the NHS. Selin is serving a complex duck dish using ingredients from all around the world to honour `Our Global NHS. And James is presenting five cuts of goat cooked in five different ways, for his bold dish `Under The Knife.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Selin puts her own unique twist on tea and toast. James creates a decadent coconut parfait accompanied by mango puree filled doughnuts for his dessert `Tree of Life inspired by the coconut palm tree, the NHS and both of their contributions to our health. Scott hopes his elevated gypsy tart `Honey Bee Good will secure him a place in the final.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining London and the South East chefs must cook their four-course menus again, but will they take on board their veterans advice as they re-make their `Feast To Say Thank You?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Vying to be crowned champion of Scotland are three talented newcomers to the GBM kitchen. Lorna McNee is a former Scottish Chef of the Year and is renowned for her technical precision which she has gained from working her entire career at 2 Michelin-starred restaurant Andrew Fairlie at Gleneagles.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and the chefs are all out to impress. The heat is increased as all three serve their unique takes on lobster. Ross is hoping to snatch victory with his dish `Lobster Aid. Lornas `Live Well, Live Very Well features only three elements, each of which she needs to deliver perfection.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Ross is hoping to impress with `Oh Deer Its Time For Your Penicillin, his tribute to Scottish scientist Alexander Fleming featuring an unusual yeast element as well as venison. Across the kitchen, Lorna, a former Game Chef of the Year, is also cooking venison in a bid to impress.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert day and the last chance for the chefs to impress before one is sent home. Ross is attempting to elevate a comfort classic into a dish worthy of the banquet. Lornas cooking a personal tribute to the NHS staff who have cared for her during treatment for a rare blood condition, using unusual yuzu and tonka bean flavours. Once again Ben is serving an experimental dish with his `Dance for the Greater Good which includes a bees wax ice cream.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its judging day and the two remaining chefs must impress the panel of Oliver Peyton, Andi Oliver and Matthew Fort. Both chefs are gunning for success and are doing everything they can to deliver their menus to perfection.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three chefs compete in the heat for the central region. Returner Ryan Simson-Trotman, who failed to reach the national finals last year, is originally from Nuneaton and opened his own restaurant Orwells in the Oxfordshire countryside with his partner Liam, who is also a contestant in the North West region.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and the chefs are pulling out all the stops. Marianne pays tribute to the `angels in her local hospital who looked after her during a recent illness. Ryans dish is a reference to the purported health benefits of fish high in omega 3 oils. While Sabrina is inspired by her aunt, a retired nurse who used to take in food to share with her fellow workers during her shifts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their mains and following yesterdays results, tension is high. Both Marianne and Ryan are cooking muntjac in the hope of impressing the veteran judge, while Sabrina is making an ambitious technical take on a family feast.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With one dish left to impress the veteran judge, the chefs cook their desserts. Marianne is looking to raise a smile with her `carry on themed dessert, complete with surprise presentation. Sabrina hopes to make it through with an elevated take on jelly and ice cream while Ryan goes all out with his very sweet treat `A Spoonful of Sugar.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining central region chefs do battle for a place in the national finals. They must impress the formidable panel of Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton with their four course menus.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Andrew is attempting to pay tribute to NHS staff with his innovative take on a traditional healing recipe, `Mums Flu Fighting Chicken Soup. Jasons `A Tea From The Heart is a play on a post-war remedy which harks back to the era when the NHS was formed. And Chris is hoping to impress with his dish inspired by a therapeutic hospital garden featuring foraged herbs from the Welsh valley where he lives including wild chervil, pickled pine buds and yarrow tips.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: After the starter results, the chefs return to the kitchen hoping for high scores with their fish dishes. Chris once again is using ingredients he has foraged from around his restaurant, while Jason hopes to impress with a spicy scallop dish and Andrew prepares a cod dish with 11 different elements.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are pulling out all the stops in a bid for big scores with their mains. All three dishes pay tribute to the founder of the NHS Aneurin Bevan, Chriss `Everything But The Squeal is an ambitious pork sharing platter, while Jasons `For The Rich And The Poor showcases lamb, and Andrew is hoping to impress with `Aneurin Bevan For Better Steak.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the dessert course and the last opportunity for the chefs to impress. Chris is hoping to triumph with another dish which showcases the foraged ingredients available around his restaurant, while Jason attempts a technical feat in his dessert inspired by a childhood hospital stay and Andrew is inspired by a dish by his great grandmother used to cook. Which two will make it through to the judging chamber?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the Wales regional final as the two remaining chefs do everything they can to impress the judging panel of Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. After tensions ran high in the week, the chefs are under pressure to deliver perfection. The judges are joined by Aneira Thomas, the first baby born on the NHS, who is named in tribute to the services founder Aneurin Bevan and who went on to dedicate her life to the service. Who will win a place in the national finals?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the turn of three chefs representing the South West, including two who have competed before but meet in the kitchen this week for the very first time.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and with Tom renowned as a fish specialist all eyes are on him. Hes cooking his take on a classic British fish supper in tribute to a charity which looks after the health of local fishermen. Jude is attempting a complex fusion dish featuring four different fish and an unusual X Ray themed presentation, while Olivia is paying tribute to the staff who looked after her during the birth of her first son with her take on curried monkfish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the main course and two of the chefs are hoping duck will win the day. Tom is preparing a technically challenging duck dish steeped in NHS history while Jude is making a multi-element Chinese duck dish inspired by his mum. Olivia is hoping her dish, using two unusual cuts of beef, will score top points. Olivia also visits her local childrens hospital, home of the second busiest paediatric AandE in the country.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the main course and two of the chefs are hoping duck will win the day. Tom is preparing a technically challenging duck dish steeped in NHS history while Jude is making a multi-element Chinese duck dish inspired by his mum. Olivia is hoping her dish, using two unusual cuts of beef, will score top points. Olivia also visits her local childrens hospital, home of the second busiest paediatric AandE in the country.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With just one more chance to impress their veteran judge, the chefs are all hoping to use their pastry skills to deliver technically complex desserts. Toms cooking an `Invalid Fruit Tart inspired by a 1950s recipe, while Jude is making six different apple elements for his `An Apple A Day. Olivia is using stout in reference to an outdated old wives tale which suggested the drink was a healthy source of iron during pregnancy.

programme.title: Rage at Dawn
programme.desc: Based on the true-life exploits of the infamous Reno Brothers gang. The film opens with a planned bank raid going wrong; in revenge, the gang find and kill the private detective who set the ambush. Undeterred, the agency send their finest man James Barlow to Indiana where, posing as a train robber, he infiltrates the gang, but can he outwit the Reno Brothers as well as keeping his identity secret from the townsfolk?

programme.title: Air Rescue
programme.desc: Documentary series following the high-stakes work and real-life drama of the crews in the Westpac Rescue Helicopter Service.

programme.desc: Seen as the first chapter in the ambitious journey of space exploration, twelve men walked on the surface of the moon between 1969 and 1972.

programme.title: Space Shuttle: The Final Mission
programme.desc: As NASA prepares to draw its shuttle curtain, Kevin Fong is granted extraordinary access to the astronauts and ground crew as they prepare for their final mission. As well as being in mission control as the astronauts go through their final launch simulation and flying with the last shuttle commander as he undertakes his last practice landing flight, Kevin gains privileged access to the shuttle itself.

programme.title: Moonwalkers: The Men of Apollo
programme.desc: Twelve astronauts discuss their personal views on the Apollo Space Program and the future of space exploration.

programme.title: Neil Armstrong: One Small Step (Anniversary Edition)
programme.desc: A portrait of Neil Armstrong, from his childhood in Ohio to his first step on the moon. With a unique insight into his life, the film captures significant moments in his life in a moving and deeply human way.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A semi in Sutton Coldfield near Birmingham, a derelict house in a rural village near Dover in Kent and a basement without planning permission in Ipswich in Suffolk are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A run-down listed village house in Farningham in Kent is given the wow factor while houses in Great Barr in Birmingham and Crewe in Cheshire are also given great makeovers. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and catch up on their progress after the transformations.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy talks to a pastor and his wife who plan to build their dream eco house in Brompton in Kent. Meanwhile, Martin meets a first-time developer in Wolverhampton, and Dion looks at a huge building which already comes with a tenanted flat and a hair salon in Newcastle-under-Lyme.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A commercial and residential building in Beeston in Nottinghamshire, a four-storey house in East Dulwich in London and a flat in Godalming in Surrey are all given high-end makeovers. Will the developers, who all bought at auction, make a profit?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A 1930s semi in Hatfield in Hertforshire, a flat in Croydon and a workshop and land in Heanor in Derbyshire are sold at auction. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the owners and hear their plans to make money from their bricks and mortar.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A mum and dad buy a piece of land in Stevenage in Hertfordshire in the hope of developing a pair of houses for their children, a couple who buy a top-floor flat in Motherwell in Scotland want to make a profit on the rent, and two young brothers buy a run-down house in South Normanton.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A bungalow in Langton Green in Kent is given a high-end refurbishment and extension, a father-and-son team cleverly refigure the existing space in a terraced house in Stoke-on-Trent, and two lifelong friends have high hopes for a piece of land they buy behind a house they already own in Northwood in Middlesex.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A 1930s semi in Leamington Spa in Warwickshire, a terraced house in Londons Plumstead and a workshop on the Isle of Sheppey in Kent are all sold under the hammer. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin hear about the plans for the three properties.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts catches up on ambitious plans to redevelop the site of a former school in Ebbw Vale in Wales, while Dion Dublin talks to a first-time developer planning to extend up and out at a semi in Bloxwich in the West Midlands.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat in Sutton in London, a studio apartment in Chatham in Kent and a terraced house in Middlesbrough are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell joins the team, and looks at an old mews house in an historic part of Litchfield in Staffordshire, while Martin Roberts assesses the potential of a bungalow in Hanley in Stoke-on-Trent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.desc: A large semi in New Barnet in London is bought as an ambitious project to turn into a family home, a family do up a semi in Dover in Kent to make a profit, and two friends club together to try their hand at refurbishing a terraced house in Derby.

programme.desc: A cold morning in December starts off like any other, then a farmer finds the body of a dead woman; what seems like a rape and murder case turns into a conspiracy, and someones diabolical nature is soon revealed.

programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a special edition of the iconic game show as Olympic silver medallist Katarina Johnson-Thompson goes up against the infamous Banker - supported on the wings by her friends, family and fellow athletes, can Katarina choose the right boxes and resist the Bankers tempting cash offers until just the right moment in order to take home a fortune for her chosen charity?

programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A prostitute sparks a murder hunt when she tells Kersey that one of her clients confessed to killing another prostitute, but how believable is she?

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: An unreliable informant claims a man was pushed off a cliff, and a re-enactment of the crime arouses suspicion that there was indeed foul play.

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Lewis and Hathaway investigate the murder of a German history professor who had a fascination with local underworld boxing bouts. When they learn that one of his students was the latest winner of such a fight, they suspect the murder may have had underworld connections.

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A rape case appears to be solved when the chief suspect is murdered. However, nothing is what it seems, and soon Lewis and Hathaway find the upper echelons of Oxford society closing ranks.

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Martin disagrees with the holistic approach to care advocated by the villages midwife, and is incensed when her advice to Louisa results in an adverse reaction. Meanwhile, Bert is smitten with the restaurants new cook, but is devastated to learn that she has a husband. Elsewhere, Edith has dinner with consultant Robert Dashwood, and tells Martin that he will be receiving some good news about the London job.

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Rumours are rife about Martins plans to leave Portwenn and take up a surgeons job in London. An insecure Pauline confronts him about her future and Martin offers to write her a reference, but it all ends in tears. At the surgery, a worried Joan Norton brings in Ted to be examined, suspecting he may have had a heart attack, and Martin attends a medical conference at a hotel where Edith is giving a key speech.

programme.title: Rosemary and Thyme
programme.desc: When a series of suspicious-looking accidents starts befalling associates of Laura Thymes estranged husband, she teams up with visiting horticulturist Rosemary Boxer to find out who is behind them.

programme.title: Rosemary and Thyme
programme.desc: Rosemary and Laura are hired to create a wild garden at a pop stars Gothic mansion, but when the singer is found shot dead, the pair get involved in a mystery from years before.

programme.title: McDonald and Dodds
programme.desc: When a young woman dies in a busy park in broad daylight, the uncovering of her identity leads DS Dodds to confront secrets from his own past.

programme.title: Agatha Christies Marple
programme.desc: When a murder is announced in the personal columns of the Chipping Cleghorn Gazette, is it just a game? The curious villagers assemble at Lettie Blacklocks house to find that their host did not place the advert. Then the room goes dark and three gun shots ring out. Lettie is injured and the man who fired the gun is dead.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When Nick Cheyney, director of a new film of `The Scarlet Pimpernel is beheaded by a guillotine on set, there is no shortage of suspects. An actor whose career he has ruined, a woman who bore his child after a drunken one-night stand, and an embittered gay, alcoholic extra all have grudges against him. Medical evidence then shows that Cheyney was drugged with morphine. Can Barnaby and Jones find the killer?

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby investigates a death which looks very much like suicide. The dead man was a troubled and depressed gambler on a losing streak, but Barnabys nose tells him to look for a murderer. During the investigation he recognises someone from his past, in the shape of Dr Jane Moore.

programme.title: Code of Silence
programme.desc: Alisons risky tactics identify the gangs target, but her deception and the gangs escalating violence threaten to unravel her relationship with Liam.

programme.title: Code of Silence
programme.desc: Alison is invited onto a lavish trip where Liam insists on adopting false identities. She discovers that he is using her as a cover. Alison is convinced that she can walk the fine line between her duty to the police and their undeniable connection.

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Olive is forced to go to work, but Stans request turns out to be an unpopular move.

programme.title: Octopussy
programme.desc: James Bond is assigned to solve the murder of agent 009, killed in East Germany clutching a fake Faberge egg. programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: A ghost ship is found on a remote island, home to one of Earths last uncontacted tribes. New evidence reveals a mysterious military compound in Russia.

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Can satellite images help identify the hiding place of the Nazi Gold Train? Also, a blood-red river in Siberia points to pollution on a catastrophic scale and a discovery of a long-lost settlement rewrites the history of the Vikings in North America.

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: A ghost ship is found on a remote island, home to one of Earths last uncontacted tribes. New evidence reveals a mysterious military compound in Russia.

programme.title: Strangest Things
programme.sub-title: The Jericho Head, The Cloudbuster And The Map
programme.desc: Examine a 9,000-year-old clay head, a 1950s machine built to control the weather, and a map which could be the oldest ever found.

programme.title: Strangest Things
programme.sub-title: The Stone Ball, The Electrocution Belt And The Cat Mummy That Wasnt
programme.desc: A 5,000-year-old carved stone object is shaped into four symmetrical lobes covered in unique markings. Why people believed an electrocution device could cure. And a 3,000-year-old cat mummy with no cat inside.

programme.title: Strangest Things
programme.sub-title: The Skull That Changed Science, The Bat Bomb And The Stone Age Mulitool
programme.desc: How an appallingly injured skull changed the face of neuroscience. Asking if World War 2 really could have been won by a bat. And investigating if people really made sophisticated multipurpose tools 14,000 years ago.

programme.title: Strangest Things
programme.sub-title: The Kings Mask, The Nazi Plate And The Stonehenge Drums
programme.desc: Exploring the origin of a death mask, three carvings which could shed light on Stonehenge, and a plate found in Austria.

programme.title: Strangest Things
programme.sub-title: The Psychograph, The Blood Mask And The Cursed Chicken Pot
programme.desc: Investigating whether a machine can read personalities. An ancient golden mask painted with human blood. And a cursed chicken pot.

programme.title: Strangest Things
programme.sub-title: The Sphere, The Prophesy Machine And The Canary
programme.desc: We find out whether Nostradamus made his predictions using a brass machine, why a diving company made a device to save a tiny bird, and how a tiny sphere can kill a man.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: On the 24th of February 1989, part of the right-side fuselage of United Airlines Flight 811 rips off, ejecting nine people from the aircraft and causing explosive decompression. The flight later lands safely at Honolulu without any more loss of life.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: Shortly after taking off from Shanghai, Korean Air Flight 6316 disappears from radar and slams into a residential neighbourhood. Investigators from China, South Korea, and the US join forces.

programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Hitlers Last Stand

programme.title: Hitlers Last Stand
programme.desc: With Hitler dead, an American Tank commander leads a race to save VIP prisoners from a castle in the Alps. But when loyal Nazis strike, they struggle to hold out.

programme.title: Erased: WW2s Heroes of Color
programme.desc: Unearthing the hidden heroes of the 320th Barrage Balloon Battalion, the only all-Black combat unit to fight on the D-Day beaches and helped liberate Europe.

programme.title: Nazi Sunken Sub
programme.desc: In 1945, the Nazi submarine U-745 mysteriously disappeared. Now a group of divers head deep below the Baltic to reveal what really happened.

programme.title: Decoding Historys Prophecies
programme.sub-title: Liu Bowen, Caesars Soothsayer, Alloa Tower
programme.desc: In 14th century China, the brilliant Liu Bowen lends his talents and prophetic predictions to a dynasty on the rise. After declaring himself dictator of Rome, Julius Caesar receives a chilling divination. Why didnt he heed the warnings?

programme.title: Pirates: Behind the Legends
programme.desc: Francis Drake is cemented in history as the worlds most controversial pirate.

programme.title: Colosseum: Rise and Fall
programme.desc: New discoveries and cutting-edge research reveal the origins of the Colosseum and how it helped Rome build an empire.

programme.title: Colosseum: Rise and Fall
programme.desc: Explores how Romes emperors used the Colosseum and its blood-soaked gladiator games to unite an empire behind symbols of Roman power.

programme.title: Colosseum: Rise and Fall
programme.desc: Experts investigate Italys Colosseum - the largest ancient amphitheatre - to understand the true nature of gladiatorial games and their role in the rise and fall of the Roman empire.

programme.title: The Lost Tomb of Alexander the Great
programme.desc: Archaeologist Pepi Papakosta is on a mission to find Alexander the Greats lost tomb. Excavating in the centre of Alexandria, Egypt, she has discovered a rare statue of Alexander and secret tunnels.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: The crash of a Korean cargo plane outside London baffled investigators. Was the first officer more concerned with saving face than the plane?

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: EgyptAir Flight 804 suddenly spirals into the Mediterranean Sea. Although early evidence suggests a fire broke out on board, Egyptian authorities say it was an act of terror.

programme.desc: Explore 125 years of aviation by uncovering how iconic aircraft have radically reshaped our world and revealing the ingenious engineering behind its success.

programme.title: The Adventures of Paddington
programme.sub-title: Paddingtons Pirate Treasure Hunt; Paddingtons Sandcastle Showstopper
programme.desc: Paddington leads a treasure hunt to find Captain Green Beards loot, but will they all remain friends and share? Everyone is keen to win the sandcastle competition and enjoy a special ice cream prize, but teamwork is the key!

programme.title: The Adventures of Paddington
programme.sub-title: Paddingtons Puffling Rescue; Paddington Goes Under the Sea
programme.desc: Paddington and the Browns rescue pufflings, who get lost around the campsite instead of taking their first flight. Paddington enjoys a diving adventure with Jonathan and Judy - but where is her necklace?

programme.title: The Adventures of Paddington
programme.sub-title: Paddingtons Beach Clean Up; Paddingtons Holiday Farewell
programme.desc: The beach is a mess. Will all the litter be cleared in time for the beach party that everyone has been looking forward to? The summer holidays have come to an end, so its time to say goodbye to their new friends.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: A storm cancels Peppa and her familys adventure plans so they decide to take it inside. Using their imagination, they go sledding, climb a mountain and have a campfire in the attic.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and George are playing in their pool. They help the garden animals cool down but when it rains, the animals are happy and so is Peppa. Rain makes muddy puddles for jumping in!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and George are in Granny and Grandpa Pigs Garden. Grannys friends are playing folk music! They play music for Grandpa Pig and then Grandpa Pig makes apple juice for everyone!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: It is bedtime and Peppa calls Kylie Kangaroo and is surprised to learn that it is not morning or winter where she is! Daddy Pig explains this using a big ball to represent the Earth.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: While visiting Suzy and Emilys house, Peppa is surprised that there are extra rules which she does not have in her own house. On the drive home, Peppa explains to Mummy Pig that different houses have different rules.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: A curious, naive, yet friendly little pig navigates through her life with her friends and family by her side. Guided by her loving mother and brash father, Peppa is stuck almost every day. Her little siblings, however, look up to Peppa lovingly.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and family are on the search for a new home.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and family renovate their home.

programme.desc: After learning about a bunch of eccentric holidays like Rock Day, Paper vows to get a holiday of his own; Scissors goes deep into his mind to decide on whether to help his roommates bring in the groceries.

programme.title: Rock Paper Scissors
programme.desc: Scissors blindly sells his likeness to a new company - which turns out to sell nappies for babies with huge poops; Rock tries to convince Paper and Scissors hes ready for a pet by taking care of a deadly robotic dog.

programme.title: Rock Paper Scissors

programme.title: King Arthurs Britain: The Truth Unearthed
programme.desc: In the fifth century, the future of Britain hung in the balance, after four centuries of straight roads and hot and cold running water the Romans upped and left, called back to support their own ailing Empire. The country quickly descended into chaos, plunging the native population into poverty and instability as their livelihoods, many dependent on the Romans, disappeared almost overnight. The nation was vulnerable and it didnt take long for Anglo-Saxon invaders to take advantage.

programme.title: The Great Fire
programme.desc: Dan Jones visits the exact location of the bakery where it started, Suzannah Lipscomb reveals how Londoners tried to save their belongings, and Rob Bell investigates 17th-century building materials and the prevailing weather conditions.

programme.title: The Great Fire
programme.desc: Dan Jones follows the path of the fire on the worst day of its rampage as it swept through some of Londons iconic buildings. Suzannah Lipscomb finds evidence in the archives of who was blamed and who escaped scot free. Rob Bell visits Imperial College London for more information about the spread of the fire and to re-create the intense heat it generated.

programme.title: The Great Fire
programme.desc: Dan Jones reveals why Londoners were desperate to stop the fire from reaching the Tower, Rob Bell looks at the bizarre 17-century methods of treating burns and Suzannah Lipscomb examines a controversial new theory about how many people died. They also examine how the authorities dealt with the 100,000 people who had lost everything and how a new city was built from the ashes.

programme.title: King Arthurs Britain: The Truth Unearthed

The country quickly descended into chaos, plunging the native population into poverty and instability as their livelihoods, many dependent on the Romans, disappeared almost overnight. The nation was vulnerable and it didnt take long for Anglo-Saxon invaders to take advantage.

programme.title: Great Forts of the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Exploring how Crusaders, Ottomans, Venetians, Moors, and European empires all built great fortresses in the Mediterranean that would witness epic battles and historic moments.

programme.title: Britney Spears - Live from London

programme.title: The Lumineers: Live At Musilac 2017
programme.desc: The Lumineers bring energetic American folk-rock to the picturesque Musilac Festival. One will not be able to resist clapping along and stomping their feet to the bands raw sound and enchanting music.

programme.title: Hall and Oates - Live at The Sydney Entertainment Centre
programme.desc: Daryl Hall and John Oates wisely reunited a few years ago after a string of solo projects, and they put on the kind of show that makes it hard to believe they even considered going their separate ways. Here they are at the Sydney Entertainment Centre.

programme.title: Ben Harper and Relentless 7 - Live at Metro Theatre
programme.desc: A natural-born showman, a slide-guitar legend, Ben Harper accompanied by the Relentless7 at the Metro Theatre in Sydney.

programme.title: Alice in Chains - Unplugged
programme.desc: In their first live concert in over 2 years (and possibly their last concert ever), Alternative/metal group Alice in Chains performs acoustically at MTV Unplugged. Includes acoustic versions of their hits.

programme.title: AC/DC - Circus Krone
programme.desc: Filmed at the Circus Krone in Munich, this concert features performances of "Rock N Roll Damnation" and "Whats Next to the Moon" and more.

programme.title: Eric Clapton and Steve Winwood - Live from Madison Square Garden
programme.desc: Eric Clapton and Steve Winwood perform at Madison Square Garden.

programme.title: Rod Stewart - Live at Hyde Park
programme.desc: A concert by Rod Stewart, that closed Radio 2s Annual Festival in a day, held in Londons Hyde Park.

programme.title: Walk the Moon: Live in London 2012
programme.desc: Walk the Moon performs live at the Roundhouse in London.

programme.title: The Moody Blues - Days of Future Passed Live
programme.desc: Celebrating the 50th anniversary of their landmark album, Days of Future Passed, The Moody Blues took to the road to perform the entire LP live. An electric evening at The Sony Centre, Toronto, where the band was joined on stage by a full orchestra for the first time ever, is the definitive performance of this seminal album.

programme.title: Adele - iTunes Festival: Live in London
programme.desc: Fans at the iTunes Festival: London 2011 were blown away with the performance of English singer-songwriter Adele. This concert was recorded live on stage at The Roundhouse in London, United Kingdom, where Adele gave one of the best shows.

programme.title: Elton John - Live in London 2013
programme.desc: Filmed at the Roundhouse in London, this concert film gives fans a live experience from start to finish; Elton John performs his hits: "Tiny Dancer," "Rocket Man," "Your Song," and many more.

programme.title: Lynyrd Skynyrd: Gone With the Wind
programme.desc: A look at the lives, careers and music of the rock band, Lynyrd Skynyrd.

programme.title: Mary J. Blige - Live in London 2014
programme.desc: Singer Mary J. Blige takes the stage to perform her biggest hits for iTunes Festival at the Roundhouse in London in 2014.

programme.title: Qello Concerts Best
programme.desc: The best of Qello Concerts.

programme.title: How Its Made

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The boys head to Wokingham in Berkshire to meet professional gardener Mark, whose barns are brimful of bikes, bric-a-brac and baggage.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole, Simon OBrien and Max McMurdo travel across the UK to find unique items lost in peoples homes to fix up for a profit. This episode features 17th-century wooden beams.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry and Simon get revved up in Pembrokeshire as they explore mechanic Jamess sheds, which are crammed with classic cars, motorbikes and tractors, including a 1928 Model A Ford.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Dealers Roo Irvine and David Harper hunt for antiques to sell at auction. Starting off in Lincolnshire, they travel far and wide in their powerful - and very loud - replica 1960s AC Cobra. Using their highly tuned antiquing skills, they scour the shops in an attempt to seek out the best items their budget affords. Along the way, they step away from the antiques shops to discover unusual historical sites. David visits Hull to test out his sea legs, heading out to sea on an unexpected adventure.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Roo Irvine and David Harper are driving around Yorkshire and Country Durham in a noisy and sporty 1960s motor, seeking out antiques to sell at auction. David buys an old fishing stool in the hope of landing a big profit at the Newcastle auction. However, the joke may be on Roo, who splashes out on an unusual purchase of a parachuting clown. A detour from the antiques emporia of York sees David discover a little-known 19th-century inventor whose incredible innovations are still relevant today.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antiques aficionados Roo Irvine and David Harper are on the third leg of their road trip, cruising around the north west in their trusty chariot, a replica 1960s AC Cobra. When David finds a carved cat at the bargain price of a pound, hes confident it will make a purr-fect profit at the Dundee auction. He also takes a journey back in time to discover the horrifying environment of the workhouses that were once prevalent across the country.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Scottish lassie Roo Irvine finds herself with home advantage as shes joined by David Harper on the west coast of Scotland for this leg of their road trip. After Roos head gets stuck in an antique, she decides to stick with what she knows best: old glass. Aside from buying antiques, Roo indulges her sweet tooth and learns about gelato and why this Italian dessert is so popular in Scotland. David examines a rare personal collection that tells the story of a once-booming industry.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Its the last outing for antiques dealers Roo Irvine and David Harper, and they are on a whistle-stop tour of the Lake District, battling each other, and the elements, in a sporty classic car. Davids hunt for antiques unearths a secret map issued by the RAF, but with time ticking before the auction, Roo gambles on a Georgian longcase clock. David learns about the Cambrian sport of hound trailing, which sees dogs running for miles with only the scent of aniseed to guide them.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Paper conservator Louise Drover rescues a water-damaged pastel portrait of a young couple. Owner Simon cherishes the portrait of him and his late wife Vicky, captured whilst on holiday in their youth. It is a delicate task for Louise as she uses more moisture to tackle the stubborn water stain. Mechanical maestro Steve Fletcher takes receipt of a 1950s toy rocket that once had sparks.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Electrics expert Mark Stuckey and silversmith Brenton West join forces to repair a much-loved, sparkling chandelier that once took pride of place in a council house. Owner Suzannes mum Pat worked as a cleaner for a wealthy couple who gifted her the cherished chandelier. A constant in her own childhood, Suzanne and her daughter Grace are delighted to return to see it back to its former glory.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: First up is a pinball penny slot machine, originally at the bar of a village pub run by owner Jeannines family. From the late 1930s until the 1950s, her great-grandfather was the landlord at this local watering hole, and was then succeeded by her grandparents. Jeannine was brought up just opposite the pub, and this antique pinball machine now provides a tangible link to her predecessors.

Elen and Master Sion come up with a plan that they hope will help understand where all the eggs are disappearing too.
